TallyPrime workflow alternative

A TallyPrime alternative for businesses that want faster voucher preparation — not blind replacement.

TallyPrime is powerful and familiar to Indian accountants. Fastbooks takes a different angle: capture bills, receipts, bank rows, and voice notes early, prepare voucher drafts, and let your accountant review before anything becomes final.

Get Early Access on WhatsApp

We respect your privacy. Message opens directly in WhatsApp; no info is saved on this site.

See WhatsApp accounting

Direct answer

Is Fastbooks a TallyPrime alternative?

Fastbooks is not positioned as a direct TallyPrime replacement. It is an accounts entry preparation layer for Indian businesses that want to reduce manual voucher entry. Businesses can capture bills, receipts, voice notes and bank rows in Fastbooks, then review prepared drafts before final posting.

  • Keep accountant control.
  • Reduce daily manual data entry.
  • Capture business activity before context is lost.

Where Fastbooks fits

Where Fastbooks fits beside Tally-style workflows

Many businesses already have a Tally-style accounting habit. The pain is usually not that accountants do not know accounting. The pain is that daily transaction context arrives late: bills are misplaced, bank narrations are unclear, staff forget cash expenses, and accountants spend time reconstructing vouchers manually. Fastbooks is built for that capture-and-prepare layer. It helps collect transaction context when it happens and turns it into review-ready accounting drafts.

Workflow comparison

Tally-style workflow vs Fastbooks workflow

Aspect Tally-style workflow Fastbooks workflow
Primary strength Strong final books, statutory familiarity, accountant adoption. Faster capture and preparation before accountant review.
Daily entry model Open desktop/accounting screen and type vouchers manually. Send bill photos, receipts, bank rows, or voice notes and prepare drafts.
Voucher preparation Party, ledger, GST, narration, and amount are selected manually. Drafts are prepared with party, ledger, GST, narration, and amount hints.
Accountant adoption Accountant controls final accounting treatment. Accountant still reviews and approves before final posting.
Bank entries Bank statement rows are interpreted manually. Bank rows can become receipt/payment drafts for review.
Best fit Businesses that want a traditional accounting system. Businesses that want to reduce daily manual voucher-preparation work.
Export or sync Final records stay in the existing accounting process. Prepare clean accounting drafts that can fit into existing accountant-led workflows; exact export/sync options should be confirmed during early access.

Solving delays

Best for businesses where Tally entry is not the problem — delayed data is

Bills come late

Bills and receipts often reach the accountant after the transaction happened. Fastbooks captures them earlier.

Bank narration needs interpretation

NEFT, UPI, IMPS, cheque, and cash movements need party and ledger context. Fastbooks prepares receipt/payment drafts for review.

Staff know context before accountants do

Owners and staff can send a quick note while the context is fresh. The accountant can verify later.

Final control stays human

Fastbooks should not blindly post accounting entries. It prepares drafts so the accountant can approve, correct, or reject.

Honest fit

Fastbooks may not be the right fit if:

You need simple billing or ERP

  • You only need simple invoice creation.
  • You want a mature full-suite accounting system today.

You want hands-off automation

  • You want a fully automatic posting system with no review.
  • Your current accounting team does not want a review-first workflow.

FAQ

TallyPrime alternative questions

Is Fastbooks a direct TallyPrime replacement?

No. Fastbooks should be understood as an entry-preparation and review layer. It helps capture daily business inputs and prepare accounting drafts. Accountants still review final treatment before anything becomes official.

Why use Fastbooks if my accountant already uses Tally?

Because the bottleneck is often before final accounting: missing bills, unclear bank entries, delayed receipts, and manual voucher preparation. Fastbooks reduces this preparation load.

Can Fastbooks prepare voucher-like entries?

Yes, the intended workflow is to prepare sales, purchase, expense, receipt, payment, and bank-entry drafts with narration, amount, party, ledger, and GST hints for review.

Can I export to Tally?

During early access, Fastbooks should be evaluated alongside your existing workflow. The goal is to support accountant-friendly handoff and export/sync workflows where practical. We do not support live automatic Tally sync at this prelaunch stage.
